items.title: 挪威将禁止小学生使用生成式人工智能
items.summary: 挪威首相斯特勒6月19日表示，为防止对学习产生负面影响，挪威将禁止小学生使用生成式人工智能工具，同时限制人工智能工具在高年级学生教育中的使用。根据挪威政府公布的方案，6到13岁的小学生原则上不得使用人工智能工具；14到16岁的初中生可在教师的严密监管下谨慎使用此类工具。17到19岁的高中生应学习如何恰当地使用人工智能工具，以便为后续的高等教育和未来的职场环境做好准备。（央视新闻）

items.title: NYU finance professor Damodaran warns an AI crash could hit harder than the dot-com bust
items.summary: NYU finance professor Aswath Damodaran believes a potential AI crash would be more painful than the bursting of the dot-com bubble because the industry is building massive amounts of debt-financed physical infrastructure rather than lightweight software. Even if AI succeeds, he sees a problem. The actual business model is to replace entire jobs, with unclear consequences for society. The article NYU finance professor Damodaran warns an AI crash could hit harder than the dot-com bust appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Data2Story turns a CSV file into a verified interactive news article using seven AI agents
items.summary: Seven AI agents work together like a newsroom. The "Data Journalist Agent" from Oxford and Stanford turns a CSV file into a finished interactive article with graphics, web research, and verifiable source links for 93 percent of all statements. In a reader study, 74 percent preferred the agent's output over the human original. But against elaborately crafted long-form reports, the agent managed a tie. The article Data2Story turns a CSV file into a verified interactive news article using seven AI agents appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Norway bans generative AI tools in elementary schools to protect kids' basic learning skills
items.summary: Norway is banning generative AI tools in elementary schools starting in late August. Prime Minister Stoere says children must first "learn to read, write, and do math." items.title: 中山大学联合团队首次实时观察到碲纳米线在液体中的生长全过程，铋籽晶调控成核与沉积效率
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，中山大学联合北京理工大学、英国曼彻斯特大学的科研人员，利用液相透射电子显微镜，首次实时记录了半导体碲纳米结构在液体环境中的成核与生长全过程。碲是一种窄带隙半导体材料，在电子器件、热电转换和光电器件领域具有应用前景，其性能高度依赖于纳米结构的尺寸与形貌。相关成果已于 6 月 18 日发表在学术期刊《Matter》上。▲ 艺术渲染图，展示了碲纳米线在溶液中的电沉积生长过程液相合成法虽然具备可规模化生产和成本相对较低的优点，但此前科学家一直难以直接观察碲的各向异性纳米结构在液相中如何起始形成并演化。此次研究借助液相扫描透射电子显微镜，对碲纳米结构的电沉积过程进行了原位实时成像。观测表明，碲最初以球形晶种颗粒的形式从溶液中析出，随后这些晶种作为模板生长出多根纳米线。在生长过程中，邻近的纳米线会互相竞争溶液中的可用碲原料，从而影响各自的局部生长速度和分支模式。定量测量显示，在不同电子束辐照条件和邻近结构影响下，纳米线的局部生长速率约为每秒 1 至 15 纳米。研究还在早期阶段观测到奥斯特瓦尔德熟化现象，为理解局部化学环境、电子束通量与形貌演化之间的关联提供了机制框架。研究还发现，向体系中添加铋纳米颗粒作为籽晶会显著改变碲的生长机制。在显微镜实验中，铋的加入增加了成核位点的数量，并促使碲形成更多分支的蕨类状结构。后续的电沉积实验进一步证实，铋能够降低碲沉积所需的

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，据韩媒 The Elec 昨天（19 日）报道，SK 海力士罕见地启动数百人规模的芯片设计工程师招聘，令韩国芯片设计行业感到不安。业内消息人士透露，SK 海力士正通过面向新员工的滚动招聘项目招募数百名设计工程师，申请将于 6 月 23 日截止。单独针对设计岗位展开如此大规模招聘，在业内极为少见。业内担心，SK 海力士吸引的可能不只是应届毕业生，还包括已经在芯片设计公司工作一至三年的年轻工程师。半导体设计人才本就稀缺，通常需要多年培养才能独当一面。一家芯片设计公司 CEO 担心，大型企业打着应届生招聘的名义招人，实际效果可能是吸走刚进入行业不久的工程师，留下中小企业难以填补的人才缺口。韩国无晶圆厂产业协会会长金京浩表示，中小企业根本无力提供与 SK 海力士相当的薪酬待遇，“如果这次数百人招聘最终变成一个黑洞，把我们花费多年培养的工程师全部吸走，韩国芯片设计行业可能遭受严重冲击。”三星电子也在密切关注 SK 海力士此次招聘。在设备解决方案部门内部，近期围绕奖金和薪酬的不满不断增加。存储业务员工预计可以获得丰厚的绩效奖金，系统 LSI 和晶圆代工部门员工的待遇却没有得到同等提升。业内认为，这种薪酬差距可能促使系统 LSI 和晶圆代工部门的年轻设计工程师跳槽。SK 海力士近期通过媒体大力宣传此次招聘，并强调选拔标准将侧重岗位能力和成长潜力，而不是学历和传统资

items.title: 《幻兽帕鲁》发行主管：我们没必要使用生成式 AI，因为玩家不想要
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，当地时间 18 日，据外媒 GamesRadar 报道，《幻兽帕鲁》开发商 Pocketpair 发行与传播负责人约翰 · 伯克利表示，玩家对生成式 AI 的反感，本身就是游戏公司不去用的充分理由。“如果玩家不想要，那大概就到此为止了，对吧？也没什么可讨论的。”伯克利此前已经否认 Pocketpair 使用生成式 AI 制作《幻兽帕鲁》的生物或其他素材。这次，他进一步确认，Pocketpair 完全没有使用生成式 AI。“我相信确实有人在用，但生成式 AI 不适合我们。我个人无法想象生成式 AI 会变得多么主流。或许有些大公司正在用生成式 AI‘节省时间’，但我不知道这种趋势还能持续多久。我不想直说生成式 AI 就是泡沫，不过现在，就连 Steam 也已经开始在一定程度上限制生成式 AI 了。”伯克利表示，即便不考虑生成式 AI 经常引发的争议，Pocketpair 也没有使用生成式 AI 的必要。“我们内部有很多美术人员，他们愿意亲自完成这些工作。为了让 AI 接手而裁掉他们，没有任何理由，也完全没有意义。”据IT之家了解，此前，伯克利曾提出，生成式 AI 和配套工具的普及，或将催生一个强调“真实性”的市场。为了打消玩家疑虑，并与 AI 生成作品划清界限，开发者可能会主动宣传“游戏 100% 由人类制作”。“我不知道我们现在是否需要开始声明，游戏

items.title: NASA 测试新一代月球 / 火星探测车原型 ERNEST：含主动悬挂与 AI 强化学习系统，最高时速 1km/h 超出毅力号、好奇号一个数量级
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，当地时间 6 月 18 日，NASA 公布了其四轮巡视器原型车的最新研发进展。据介绍，这辆原型车于 2026 年 3 月在加州科罗拉多沙漠完成了一项野外测试，在工程师仅提供最低限度干预的情况下行驶了约 26 公里。该原型车名为 ERNEST（Exploration Rover for Navigating Extreme Sloped Terrain，用于极端陡坡地形导航的探索巡视器），正在被 NASA 用来推进机器人自主能力和崎岖地形穿越能力的研发。ERNEST 长 1.2 米，它采用了不同于“好奇号”与“毅力号”所使用六轮摇臂式（rocker-bogie）结构的设计思路，通过可升降的网状轮组与主动悬挂系统，使每个车轮能够适应复杂地形并跨越障碍。其前部配备两个动力关节，可驱动类似“蠕动”“轮式行走”以及障碍攀爬等多种运动模式。同时，该系统可在主动与被动悬挂之间切换。四轮全向转向设计也使其具备包括横向移动在内的多方向行驶能力。该项目旨在验证未来探测任务所需的高机动性与更高等级的自主驾驶能力，并为可能的月球长期远距离探测任务提供技术基础。在此次野外测试中，ERNEST 充当了一项潜在未来月球任务的测试平台，该任务所需的速度和行驶里程远超现有巡视器所能达到的水平。JPL 首席技术专家 Issa Nesnas 是此次测试的负责人，同时担任一项 NASA 远

items.title: 零跑汽车：今年 Q3 将公布多项创新技术成果
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，在 2027 款零跑 C 系列上市发布会后的媒体群访活动中，零跑汽车方面透露，公司将在今年第三季度举行“技术日”，公布多项创新技术成果。同时，更具创新性、差异化的产品也在规划中。零跑汽车创始人朱江明透露：“零跑经过长期的全域自研，打下了非常好的基础，这也为后面的发展，创造了很好的条件。我们也在十周年的发布会上，讲了今年第三季度要对外公布零跑很多创新的技术，我们还是很期待零跑的技术。零跑积累了很多技术创新的干货，能够给到大家。同时，我们也在规划零跑更有创新，更与众不同的一些产品，我相信未来的产品，一定是需要走创新之路，与众不同的产品，才可以走出更美好的一片天地，我想零跑未来的十年，一定会给大家很多意想不到的惊喜。”据IT之家此前报道，今年 3 月，朱江明表示，“零跑还是沿袭原来的风格，慢热型，打后发优势。2021 年左右只有几十人的智驾团队，投入资金也很少，算力也是以租代买。现在，我们已经跟上节奏了，开始接近第一梯队的水平。2026 年，我们会全面爆发。我们有很多很多。现在要保密。再等等，下半年，大家就知道了。整车上、技术上至少有几项会让大家非常惊喜。”相关阅读：《朱江明：零跑汽车的辅助驾驶 2026 年会全面爆发，下半年整车、技术有几项惊喜》

items.title: Take-Two 前 AI 负责人：我担心生成式 AI 会败坏整个 AI 领域的声誉
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，当地时间 19 日，据外媒 Eurogamer 报道，Take-Two 曾设有专门研究 AI 如何帮助游戏开发的内部团队，负责人是卢克 · 迪肯博士。然而，Take-Two 据报已在今年 4 月裁撤整个 AI 研究团队。在越来越多游戏公司公开或暗中把生成式 AI 纳入开发流程之际，Take-Two 的决定显得颇为意外。不过，被裁员工大多并不负责生成式 AI，而是在研究更广泛的 AI 技术。据IT之家了解，团队最早于 2019 年在手游开发商 Zynga 内部成立。Take-Two 在 2022 年收购 Zynga 后，又把研究范围扩大到整个公司。迪肯接受 GamesIndustry.biz 采访时表示，这个内部创新研发团队成立时，ChatGPT 尚未面向公众推出，生成式 AI 热潮也还没有出现。“我从来没有特别热衷于生成式 AI。我认为，我们有道德责任尽可能妥善管理生成式 AI。不过，对 2025 年或 2026 年的任何大企业来说，完全拒绝生成式 AI 都不是正确答案，也一定会引起很多人的反感。”迪肯认为，生成式 AI 确实容易造成严重分歧，“但有些生成式 AI 应用已经过分到令人无法接受，因此必须有人站出来反对。”相比只把目光放在生成式 AI 上，迪肯更希望行业重新认识传统 AI 技术的价值。“五年前，如果我们告诉别人，有一种算法可以大幅加快手游

items.title: 纽约大学金融学教授达摩达兰警告：AI 泡沫破裂后果恐比互联网泡沫更严重
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，据外媒 The Decoder 今天（20 日）报道，纽约大学金融学教授阿斯沃斯 · 达摩达兰警告称，如果 AI 行业泡沫破裂，冲击可能比 2000 年前后的互联网泡沫更严重。达摩达兰认为，AI 热潮与互联网泡沫存在一个关键区别：AI 行业需要投入巨额资金建设数据中心等实体基础设施，而且相当一部分投资依靠举债完成。一旦市场大幅调整，损失不会仅由股东承担，还可能向整个社会扩散。达摩达兰还怀疑，AI 商业模式能否实现外界期待的规模效应。他认为，AI 与传统软件不同，用户越多并不意味着单位成本会自然趋近于零。每增加一次使用，AI 都要消耗更多算力，商业模式更接近每次播放都要支付费用的 Spotify。IT之家从报道中获悉，相比之下，Netflix 可以把高额内容成本分摊给不断增长的订阅用户，而 Spotify 每增加一次播放都会产生新的成本。因此，AI 的规模经济可能远弱于传统软件业务。若企业在利润率微薄的情况下盲目追求增长，最终甚至可能毁掉价值。DeepSeek 等中国竞争对手还可能继续压低价格，而 AI 行业当前的利润率已经不高。达摩达兰表示，即便最乐观的 AI 叙事最终成为现实，同样可能带来严重后果。因为在这种情景下，AI 企业出售的将不再只是辅助工具，而是能够直接取代完整岗位的系统。如果 AI 真能实现这一目标，一半白领员工可能失去工作。若足以支撑

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，育碧联合创始人 Claude Guillemot 于当地时间 6 月 19 日傍晚在法国西部拉博勒附近遭遇飞机失事，不幸去世，享年 69 岁。据大西洋卢瓦尔省消防部门通报，一架从雷恩起飞的双引擎塞斯纳 421 螺旋桨飞机在接近拉博勒机场时坠入一片田地，机上两名乘客全部遇难。事发时间约为 17 时 50 分，飞机在偏离降落轴线的位置坠毁，残骸起火，约 61 名消防员抵达现场后迅速控制住了火势。Claude Guillemot 是这架飞机的所有者，同时也是拉博勒飞行俱乐部成员。法媒报道称，他在此次飞行中亲自操控飞机，同机另有一名来自雷恩的飞行教员。由于遗体识别工作尚未完成，正式身份确认仍需等待，但 Guillemot 家属已于事发当晚接到通知。公开资料显示，Claude Guillemot 与其四位兄弟 Christian、Gérard、Michel 和 Yves 共同创立了育碧。1980 年代初，Guillemot 家族在法国布列塔尼地区经营农用物资生意，五兄弟在完成商业与技术相关学业后，意识到传统农业业务的增长空间有限，转而投向新兴的电子产品市场。Claude 最初尝试销售音频 CD，但几兄弟很快发现更广阔的机会在于快速扩张的家用电脑市场。由于从英美进口硬件和软件对法国消费者而言价格高昂，他们创立了邮购公司 Guillemot Informatiqu

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，1966 年 7 月 1 日，中国战略导弹部队“第二炮兵”正式组建。60 年后，这支从戈壁荒原起步的战略力量已发展为核常兼备、全域慑战的火箭军。今日，央视新闻《军情时间到》首次披露了我国东风-17 高超音速导弹实战化演练的诸多细节。在中国西北戈壁深处的一场多军兵种联合训练中，火箭军某旅与陆军、空军等多支作战力量同步编入训练体系，全方位磨炼合同训法。与此同时，在火箭军某野外训练场，数枚新型导弹正在起竖。央视报道称，各作战单元不仅要密切协同完成多波次的火力打击，还要随时做好防强电磁干扰、防精确打击等特情处置。据参训官兵介绍，我国新型装备智能化程度更高，机动性能更强，火力打击更加精确，具有全天候、无依托、强突防的作战优势。新装备的列装使得指挥链路更加精简，极大缩短了形成火力的时间，同时也打破了地域、阵地、气象等条件的限制，使得一大批紧贴战场的战法训法不断涌现，真正实现了从固定点火到随机发射、从定点备战到全域慑战的跨越。在另外一处，火箭军各单元正在按照作战编组有序机动，全程保持巡逻待战状态，可以随时根据指令切换作战模式。对于火箭军官兵来说，这种高强度、多军兵种协同的实战化训练已成为常态。军事评论员杜文龙在央视节目中表示，这是第一次看到东风-17 的发射状态，从短片所呈现的状态来看，火箭军的实战标准非常高。他进一步分析了三个方面的亮点：首先是野外条件，发射车在

items.title: 高通 CEO 安蒙：智能眼镜市场有望比肩手机
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，当地时间 19 日，据美国 CNBC 报道，高通 CEO 安蒙认为，AI 智能体将改变人们使用应用和电子设备的方式。高通的芯片广泛用于各类消费电子设备，安蒙设想的未来入口之一，是内置摄像头和显示屏的智能眼镜。比如，用户走在路上时突然想起需要预订餐厅，不必再拿出手机搜索推荐地点，只需告诉数字助理自己的需求。数字助理随后调用餐厅预订、支付和电子邮件等多个应用，完成预订并发送确认信息。安蒙认为，AI 智能体可以协调不同应用完成整个流程。应用本身不会消失但会发生变化，智能体将成为新的应用。多年来，智能手机不断进化，不仅出现折叠屏形态，摄像头性能也越来越强。不过，苹果 Siri、三星 Bixby 等数字助理始终没有完全兑现最初的设想。随着 AI 模型持续进步，数字助理的能力与日俱增。安蒙表示，未来的手机和新型设备都会围绕 AI 智能体重新设计，“手机将围绕智能体打造，各类新设备也会围绕智能体打造。”AI 智能体还可能推动新一轮硬件创新。安蒙表示，高通目前正在参与 40 多种 AI 设备的设计，其中涵盖了智能首饰、带摄像头的耳机、智能胸针和手表。“基本原则是，设备要能够佩戴在身上，始终陪伴用户，并且可以观察周围世界。设备掌握环境信息后，用户就能随时调用智能体并与智能体交流。”在各种新设备中，安蒙目前最看好智能眼镜。他预计，智能眼镜最终可能发展到与智能手机相当的规

items.title: 调查显示：美国成年人年均订阅支出 1332 美元，未使用服务年均浪费 252 美元
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，据 CNET 最新发布的《2026 年订阅服务现状》调查报告，美国消费者在各类订阅服务上的支出仍在持续增长。调查显示，在过去一年内曾购买订阅服务的美国成年人，平均每月订阅支出达 111 美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 753.1 元人民币），全年约为 1332 美元（现汇率约合 9037 元人民币），同比增长约 23%。与此同时，受访者平均每年有 252 美元（现汇率约合 1710 元人民币）花在几乎“从来不用”的订阅项目上，高于上一年的 204 美元。调查显示，流媒体视频仍是美国最常见的订阅类型，61% 的受访者表示过去一年曾订阅 Netflix、Hulu、Prime Video 等视频平台；零售会员服务位居第二，占比为 48%，包括开市客（Costco）等会员体系；音乐订阅排名第三，占比 35%，涵盖 Spotify、Apple Music 等平台。相比之下，AI 服务、家庭安防应用、软件订阅以及预制餐配送等订阅项目的用户比例相对较低。CNET 指出，随着影视、体育赛事版权分散到不同平台，消费者往往需要同时订阅多个服务才能观看感兴趣的内容，这也进一步推高了整体订阅开销。从不同年龄层来看，千禧一代平均每月订阅支出最高，达到 125 美元；X 世代最低，为 100 美元。另一方面，闲置订阅带来的浪费同样集中在年轻群体，千禧一代平均每月浪费 29 美元，

items.title: 英国科学家攻克量子传感器噪声难题，有助于探索暗物质与早期宇宙引力波
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，据新华社今日报道，英国帝国理工学院牵头的一支科研团队首次在接近实际应用的实验条件下验证了一项新型量子传感技术，通过比较两台原子干涉仪的测量结果，有效消除了激光带来的背景噪声，即使单次测量信号被噪声淹没，系统仍能恢复出真实信号。这项成果为未来建设大规模量子传感器奠定了技术基础，为捕捉极早期宇宙的引力波信号和难以探测的暗物质踪迹铺平了道路。▲ 在将原子冷却至接近绝对零度之前，对蓝色激光的偏振进行调整该研究属于英国原子干涉仪观测站与网络（AION）计划的一部分，由英国帝国理工学院牵头，联合伯明翰大学、剑桥大学、利物浦国王学院、牛津大学以及英国科学技术设施委员会卢瑟福 · 阿普尔顿实验室的学者共同完成，其成果被视作长基线原子干涉仪从理论设想走向实际建造的关键一步。据研究人员介绍，现代物理学仍面临诸多尚未解决的问题，例如暗物质究竟由什么组成、宇宙中是否存在新的引力波来源等，而这些研究都需要探测极其微弱的物理信号。原子干涉仪利用激光操控原子云，并通过原子物质波之间的干涉现象实现高精度测量，是目前最具潜力的量子探测技术之一。然而，这类实验依赖激光脉冲完成测量，而激光本身产生的相位噪声远大于目标信号，往往会掩盖真正需要观测的数据，成为限制相关技术发展的主要障碍。▲ 帝国理工学院实验室用于冷却原子和操控其量子态的激光系统之一为解决这一问题，研究团队提出并验证了差分测量

items.title: 中国科学院微电子所突破 3D DRAM 技术：首次展示 4 层 3D 2T0C，数据保持 400 秒
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，中国科学院微电子研究所 6 月 17 日发布消息称，集成电路制造技术全国重点实验室团队联合北京超弦设备研究院，在基于 IGZO（铟镓锌氧化物）的 2T0C 三维动态随机存取存储器（3D DRAM）研究方面取得新进展，并提出基于 2T0C 单元结构的单步高层三维集成方案，首次展示了四层 3D 2T0C 结构。相关成果论文《Highly stackable 3D DRAM of Dual-gate IGZO 2T0C with Record 3 bits / cell and 400s Data Retention》已入选 2026 IEEE Symposium on VLSI Technology and Circuits（VLSI 2026）。▲ 图 1 高分辨率 TEM 表征随着人工智能和高性能计算应用持续发展，业界对高容量、高带宽存储器的需求不断提升。传统 SRAM 受限于 6T 单元结构，难以兼顾更高存储容量；片外 DRAM 又会因访问延迟增加而影响带宽表现。研究团队介绍称，基于 IGZO 的 2T0C 架构可集成于逻辑芯片后道工艺之上，被认为是兼顾高容量和高带宽的一种技术路线。不过，现有 2T0C DRAM 研究主要集中于平面架构和垂直 4F² 架构，尚缺少能够实现单步多层堆叠的三维集成方案，限制了存储密度进一步提升。此次研究正是围绕这一问题

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，据英国《卫报》报道，当地时间周三，加州官员宣布，向亿万富翁征收财富税的《加州亿万富翁税法案》已经获得足够签名，可以列入今年 11 月的投票议程。这项通常被称为“亿万富翁税”的提案，计划向净资产超过 10 亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 67.84 亿元人民币）的加州居民一次性征收 5% 财富税。提案已经成为加州最激烈的政治争议之一。一边是支持征税的工会，另一边则包括多名科技富豪和加州州长加文 · 纽森。随着提案今年获得越来越多公众支持，谷歌联合创始人拉里 · 佩奇、Meta 联合创始人马克 · 扎克伯格等亿万富翁开始减少与加州的联系，纽森则承诺阻止提案进入投票程序。签名数量达标的同时，支持提案的联盟仍有时间作出最终决定。联盟必须在 6 月 25 日前选择继续推动 11 月投票，或者与纽森方面达成协议。国际服务业雇员工会西部联合医疗工作者分会（SEIU-UHW）是主要支持者，希望用税收收入缓解加州医疗、食品援助和教育项目面临的资金压力。当地时间周四晚些时候，由 SEIU-UHW 牵头的“立即征收亿万富翁税联盟”致信纽森，提出把一次性税率从 5% 降至 2%，并要求纽森支持修改后的方案。联盟称，一次性对亿万富翁积累的财富征收 2%，按照任何客观标准都算温和，尤其是税收能够帮助急诊室维持运营并挽救患者生命，“当加州其他所有人都被州政府要求作出牺牲时，这项要

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，当地时间 18 日，据英国《卫报》报道，英格兰和威尔士总检察长理查德 · 赫默要求办公室停止在 X 发帖。随着外界越来越担心 X 被用于煽动暴力和种族主义，赫默办公室成为英国首个停用 X 平台的政府部门。赫默办公室上周五最后一次在 X 发布内容。据了解，官员今后不得再使用 X，除非是反驳平台上的虚假信息。英国政府此前多次为各部门继续使用 X 辩护。尽管马斯克曾呼吁推翻英国政府，X 也长期因传播极右翼和种族主义观点受到批评，但唐宁街认为，政府需要借助 X 接触公众。赫默办公室停用 X，是英国政府内部的态度开始转变的首个信号。IT之家从报道中获悉，随着社交媒体造成的社会影响不断扩大，英国大臣的担忧也日益加深。据了解，赫默是在本月早些时候南安普敦和贝尔法斯特发生骚乱后作出决定。赫默尤其担心，恶意行为者利用 X 煽动对立、分裂不同社区的情况，比其他社交平台更加严重。赫默理解政府其他部门继续留在 X 的理由，不过仍决定让自己的办公室退出。今年早些时候，X 旗下 Grok AI 工具生成大量涉及女性和儿童的色情化图片后，英国首相基尔 · 斯塔默警告，如果 X 不采取行动，可能会被该国政府封锁。X 随后作出整改。此外，英国政府计划修改《网络安全法》，要求社交媒体公司在骚乱或其他危机发生时更快删除煽动性内容。不过，新规定最早也要到 7 月中旬才能生效。在此之前，英国

items.title: 微信 AI 助手“小微”灰度上线，可通过文字或语音对话操作微信原生功能
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 20 日消息，微信原生 AI 助手“小微”今日开始扩大灰度测试范围，多个平台上都出现了用户反馈。腾讯客服回复IT之家称：“小微”目前仍处于测试阶段，支持通过文字或语音对话操作微信原生功能，例如调整设置、发送消息、拨打电话、点外卖、生成图片等。客服同时表示，灰度测试覆盖多个系统，具体参与资格以实际页面显示为准。微信小微（智能助手）是微信团队在小范围内测的原生 AI 助手，您可以在微信主界面左上角点击「小微」标志进入。目前该功能仍在小范围内测中，若未获得内测名额，建议您后续留意版本更新。小微支持通过文字或语音对话操作微信原生功能，调起小程序提供日常服务，还能帮助您搜索资料、生成图片等。您也可以进入【小微 > 右上角“...” > 设置 > 记忆与隐私 > 记忆】管理对话记录。如图所示，获得测试资格的用户能够在微信页面的左上角看到一个带有绿色眼睛的机器人标识，点击后即可进入“小微”界面，其顶部标注有“测试版”字样。另外，也有用户通过对话框菜单栏等路径进入了该界面。从界面形态来看，“小微”是一个对话式的 AI 助手。在功能层面，“小微”除了基础的聊天对话，还集成了文件总结、提醒设置、音乐推荐等能力。其中最受关注的功能是“一句话生成小程序”。例如，小红书用户 @iPhysicist 通过自然语言描述，让“小微”直接生成了一个具备实用性的小程序雏形。用户还可以通过多轮对话要求

items.summary: arXiv:2606.19469v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Undergraduate computer science is governed by international curricular guidelines revised about once a decade, yet programs lack a reliable, reproducible way to measure how completely they cover the current guidelines and how that coverage shifts when the guidelines are restructured. We address this with a human-in-the-loop pipeline that measures a program's coverage of an external body of knowledge, applied longitudinally to one accredited BSc in Computer Science against Computer Science Curricula 2013 (CS2013) and 2023 (CS2023). items.title: Diffusion Language Models: An Experimental Analysis
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19475v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized language modeling through autoregressive generation, enabling strong performance across a wide range of tasks. Recently, Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) have emerged as an alternative paradigm that generates text through iterative denoising rather than next-token prediction, allowing parallel refinement of entire sequences. items.title: Hidden Anchors in Multi-Agent LLM Deliberation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent LLM deliberation, where agents exchange and revise answers over several rounds, is increasingly used to improve reasoning and accuracy, yet how and why it works is rarely modelled. Such deliberation mirrors how humans reach decisions. As social animals we are pulled both by the group, the herd effect that classical opinion-dynamics models such as DeGroot and Friedkin--Johnsen capture, and by our own internal belief, which they do not. items.title: DeXposure-Claw: An Agentic System for DeFi Risk Supervision
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19501v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized finance exposes supervisors to fast-moving, networked credit risks. General-purpose LLM agents fit this setting poorly: they over-read weak evidence and recommend high-stakes interventions, while existing evaluations offer no regulator-aligned way to measure the resulting false alarms. items.title: LLM Doesn't Know What It Doesn't Know: Detecting Epistemic Blind Spots via Cross-Model Attribution Divergence on Clinical Tabular Data
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19509v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to structured clinical data, yet whether they can recognize the limits of their own knowledge on such tasks remains unexplored. We study this question through the lens of cross-model attribution divergence with the goal of reducing epistemic uncertainty for structured tasks, comparing Qwen 2.5 7B and XGBoost on a prediction task via attribution divergence analysis. items.title: REVEAL++: Differentiable Phenotypic Grouping for Vision-Language Retinal Modeling of Alzheimer's Disease Risk
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19522v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The retina offers a noninvasive window into neurodegenerative disease, capturing subtle structural patterns associated with a risk of future cognitive decline. Vision-language alignment frameworks such as REVEAL have shown that pairing retinal fundus images with structured clinical risk narratives improves early prediction of Alzheimer's disease (AD). items.title: Emergent Alignment
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19527v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can Large Language Models (LLMs) discern when their own outputs are misaligned with human ethics? And can they self-correct? We endow an LLM with a conscience step that reviews its own reasoning and outputs, and we extend the training loss with an alignment component using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to steer the model away from non-ethical outputs. items.title: ITNet: A Learnable Integral Transform That Subsumes Convolution, Attention, and Recurrence
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19538v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Convolutional networks, recurrent networks, and transformers each encode different inductive biases -- locality, sequential memory, and content-dependent pairwise interaction -- and have remained mathematically distinct since their inception. We show that this fragmentation reflects not a fundamental diversity in how signals should be processed, but rather incomplete views of a single underlying mathematical object: a learnable integral transform. items.title: Uncertainty Decomposition for Clarification Seeking in LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19559v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent position papers argue that the classical aleatoric/epistemic uncertainty framework is insufficient for interactive large language model (LLM) agents and call for underspecification-aware, decomposed, and communicable uncertainty representations that can unlock new agent capabilities such as proactive clarification seeking and shared mental-model building. items.title: Analyzing the Narration Gap in LLM-Solver Loops
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal tools such as SAT and SMT solvers are increasingly embedded in language model reasoning pipelines when a safety or security critical question can be formulated in logic. Unlike chain of thought whose steps are sampled from the model distribution without formal guarantee, a solver produces a sound and independently verifiable answer. However, the soundness guarantee can be lost in the interaction between the solver and the model. items.title: Configurable Clinical Information Extraction with Agentic RAG: What Works, What Breaks, and Why
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patient contexts span hundreds of heterogeneous documents and thousands of structured data points, yet the document-level metadata that AI systems need for retrieval and triage is absent or incomplete. Standard retrieval-augmented generation fails on this data, mishandling temporal reasoning, cross-document dependencies, and missing metadata. items.title: Which Pairs to Compare for LLM Post-Training?
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19607v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based post-training has become a central paradigm for aligning language models. A common data-collection strategy is to generate a small set of completions for each prompt and label the resulting comparison pairs. However, human preference labels are often much more expensive than generating additional completions, suggesting a different use of the same labeling budget: generate a larger pool of completions, but label only the most informative comparison pairs. items.title: Toten: Knowledge-Based Ontological Tokenization Of Physical Quantities And Technical Notation In Brazilian Portuguese
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19626v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Byte-Pair Encoding tokenization is statistically efficient for vocabulary compression, but semantically blind to structured technical entities, fragmenting physical quantities, numbers, units, and symbolic expressions into lexically arbitrary subwords. We present TOTEN, a knowledge-based ontological tokenization framework that replaces statistical derivation with declarative classification grounded in a formal ontology of engineering entities (OEE). items.title: AI4SE and SE4AI Exploration: A Decade Looking Back and Forward
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19630v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The March 2020 INCOSE INSIGHT special issue on AI and Systems Engineering (SE) became the most downloaded issue in the publication's history and launched a research community that now draws over 250 registrants to its annual workshop. In this article, we trace the progress in AI and SE across three phases (labeled here foundational, applied, and LLM inflection) based on the authors' reading of the field's core papers, and describe our opinions of where the community has converged and where critical gaps remain. items.title: BrainG3N: A Dual-Purpose Tokenizer for Controllable 3D Brain MRI Generation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) brain MRI is central to clinical neurology and neuro-oncology, where generative models could augment under-represented cohorts, simulate disease trajectories, and support privacy-preserving data sharing. Latent diffusion has been the go-to solution for modeling imaging data, but it places two competing demands on the tokenizer: encoder embeddings must retain the clinical information that downstream tasks act on, and the decoder must reconstruct anatomically faithful volumes. items.title: Denoising Implicit Feedback for Cold-start Recommendation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19658v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Implicit feedback is widely used in recommender systems due to its accessibility and generality, yet it usually presents noisy samples (e.g., clickbait, position bias). Meanwhile, recommenders inevitably face the item cold-start problem due to the continuous influx of new items. We identify that cold items are more prone to noisy samples due to the aforementioned factors, and researchers often overlook the significance of denoising implicit feedback for cold items. items.title: Exit-and-Join Dynamics for Decentralized Coalition Formation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper studies coalition formation as a decentralized dynamical process driven by unilateral exit-and-join decisions. Agents evaluate local moves using the Aumann-Dreze value, so payoffs are computed within the agent's current coalition rather than through a globally negotiated coalition structure. items.title: Beyond Static Leaderboards: Predictive Validity for the Evaluation of LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19704v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent benchmarks are growing fast, but no single benchmark touches more than four or five of the dimensions that deployment exposes. This paper aggregates the largest coordinated deep-dive of one MCP-based industrial-agent benchmark to date: fourteen parallel implementation studies covering new asset classes (including a multi-modal visual extension), alternative orchestrations, retrieval strategies, reasoning modes, infrastructure optimizations, and evaluation-methodology probes. items.title: GLARE: A Natural Language Interface for Querying Global Explanations
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19735v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While global explanations are crucial for understanding vision models across datasets, classes, and decision contexts, their complex and monolithic nature often hinders practical exploration. Because users typically seek targeted answers to specific questions rather than static artifacts, we present an LLM-based interactive interface that provides natural language access to global explanations for black-box image classifiers. items.title: Interpreting Neural Combinatorial Optimization via Evolving Programmatic Bottlenecks
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19741v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural Combinatorial Optimization (NCO) achieves strong performance, yet its black-box nature remains a key roadblock to deployment and scientific diagnosis. Standard interpretability tools, such as Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs), are ill-equipped for NCO, whose decisions are dynamic, state-dependent, and lack proper concept vocabulary definition. items.title: A Comparative Study of Pretrained Transformer Models for Quranic ASR: Speech Representations, Label Formats, and Dataset Composition
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quran Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) aims to convert Quranic recitation into text, enabling applications such as aided memorisation tools and Quranic search engines. However, existing ASR models often exhibit high Word Error Rates (WER) on user-recited verses and lack full coverage of the Quranic corpus. This paper presents a systematic empirical study of domain-specific fine-tuning of pretrained Transformer-based models for Quranic ASR, using advanced speech feature extraction methods: Wav2Vec2.0, HuBERT, and XLS-R. items.title: Benchmarking Agentic Review Systems
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19749v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A new class of agentic review systems are emerging as a remedy to the pressure placed on peer review systems by AI-assisted research, but it is unclear how they should be evaluated. We evaluate two open-source systems (OpenAIReview and coarse), one proprietary system (Reviewer3), and a zero-shot baseline, across six LLMs spanning frontier and efficient models. items.title: Grounded Inference: Principles for Deterministically Encapsulated Generative Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19753v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The incorporation of generative models into traditional computational systems presents both enormous opportunity and tremendous peril. Although many early adopters have realized these perils at great expense, the field still requires foundational frameworks to de-risk incorporation of AI into traditional systems. items.title: Optimal Scheduling in a Question-Answering Forum of Knowledge Workers
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19759v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As individuals turn to the Internet to find answers to questions they may have, several Question Answering (QA) forums have evolved, where users knowledgeable in certain topics can contribute their expertise to answering these requests for information. While these are currently volunteer based, we consider a future version employing knowledge workers who are experts in certain topics. items.title: Beyond Entropy: Learning from Token-Level Distributional Deviations for LLM Reasoning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19771v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning; however, it faces a fundamental optimization instability: uniform token updates precipitate entropy collapse, leading to premature convergence to suboptimal strategies, whereas excessive Shannon Entropy maximization can cause entropy explosion, driving blind exploration toward incoherent reasoning chains. items.title: AgentFinVQA: A Deployable Multi-Agent Pipeline for Auditable Financial Chart QA
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Financial chart question answering in regulated settings demands more than accuracy: practitioners must know which answers to trust before acting on them, and many institutions cannot send client data to external model providers. Yet existing chart-QA agents are accuracy-focused and opaque, and most assume proprietary API access; to our knowledge, none combines auditability with on-premise deployability without significant accuracy compromise. items.title: ORAgentBench: Can LLM Agents Solve Challenging Operations Research Tasks End to End?
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19787v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents for multi-step tasks in executable environments, yet their ability to perform realistic operations research (OR) work remains unclear. Existing OR evaluations often decouple modeling from solving, rely on pre-formalized or text-only instances, and rarely test the full workflow from operational artifacts to validated decisions. items.title: CombEval: A Framework for Evaluating Combinatorial Counting in Large Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19788v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present CombEval, a dynamic benchmark for evaluating combinatorial counting in large language models. CombEval represents each problem as a typed Cofola specification over entities, combinatorial objects, object dependencies, and constraints, enabling controlled generation of natural-language counting problems with exact solver-verified answers. items.title: Think Again or Think Longer? Selective Verification for Budget-Aware Reasoning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19808v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Test-time reasoning is increasingly used as a serving-time control knob, but extra reasoning is not uniformly valuable: it can repair failed attempts, waste compute on already-correct answers, or introduce harmful answer changes. We study this as a deployment allocation problem rather than a new-verifier problem. items.title: Human-on-the-Loop Orchestration for AI-Assisted Legal Discovery
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19812v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in electronic discovery (e-discovery), where compounding errors across multi-step reasoning chains can constitute legal malpractice. Unlike single-turn retrieval, agentic workflows operating over privileged document corpora exhibit a class of failure we term "trajectory collapse": an early misclassification silently propagates, rendering an entire privilege review invalid. items.title: TelcoAgent: A Scalable 5G Multi-KPM Forecasting With 3GPP-Grounded Explainability
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19821v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Key Performance Measurement (KPM) forecasting is essential for proactive network management of 5G and next-generation telecom networks. However, existing machine learning (ML) approaches face significant limitations in scalability and explainability, restricting their effectiveness in real-world deployments. items.title: A Systematic Evaluation of Black-Box Uncertainty Estimation Methods for Large Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19868v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although large language models (LLMs) have shown strong capabilities across a wide range of tasks, their outputs often remain unreliable and may contain hallucinations, making uncertainty estimation (UE) essential for building trustworthy LLMs. In practice, many mainstream LLMs are only accessible through restricted APIs, where internal signals such as logits and hidden states are unavailable, making black-box UE especially important. items.title: MetaResearcher: Scaling Deep Research via Self-Reflective Reinforcement Learning in Adversarial Virtual Environments
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19893v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep research agents have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in autonomous information gathering and synthesis, yet their training remains constrained by the static nature of simulated environments, the limits of fact-retrieval-only task designs, and the inefficiency of outcome-based reinforcement learning. In this work, we propose MetaResearcher, a novel framework that scales deep research agent training across four synergistic dimensions. items.title: Multi-Agent Transactive Memory
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19911v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The decentralized deployment of LLM agents with diverse capabilities across diverse tasks motivates infrastructure for knowledge sharing across heterogeneous agent populations. Just as search engines index human-generated artifacts to support human problem solving, retrieval systems can organize agent-generated artifacts for reuse across agent populations. items.title: eCNNTO: A Highly Generalizable ConvNet for Accelerating Topology Optimization
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19921v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work proposes an element-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to accelerate density-based Topology Optimization (TO), termed eCNNTO. TO generally undergoes a large number of iterations, where finite element analysis is performed in every iteration, leading to the efficiency bottleneck especially when dense meshes are used to achieve high-resolution designs. items.title: The Tao of Agency: Autotelic AI, Embedded Agency and Dissolution of the Self
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most artificial intelligence systems are built on the assumption that goals are exogenous and specified by the designer. Exploring what happens when an agent begins generating its own goals opens the field of autotelic AI. Agents are expected not merely to pursue objectives but to discover them. In this article, we trace its consequences through intrinsic motivation, resource-driven priors, causal-interventional learning, homeostasis, and embeddedness; the last of which is found to be a necessary but not sufficient condition for autotelic agency.

items.title: PhysDrift: Bridging the Embodiment Gap in Humanoid Co-Speech Motion Generation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19935v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humanoid robots require co-speech motions that are not only expressive and speech-aligned, but also physically executable under embodiment constraints. Existing co-speech generation pipelines are predominantly human-centric: motions are first generated in human-body representations such as SMPL-X and subsequently retargeted to humanoid robots. items.title: Advancing DialNav through Automatic Embodied Dialog Augmentation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19948v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For embodied agents capable of physical interaction, the capability to create and understand dialog is crucial to ensure both safety and effectiveness. While DialNav~\cite{han2025dialnav} provides a framework for holistic evaluation of the dialog--execution loop in photorealistic indoor navigation, its performance remains limited by a critical scarcity of training data (2K episodes). items.title: ENPIRE: Agentic Robot Policy Self-Improvement in the Real World
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Achieving dexterous robotic manipulation in the real world heavily relies on human supervision and algorithm engineering, which becomes a central bottleneck in the pursuit of general physical intelligence. Although emerging coding agents can generate code to automate algorithm search, their successes remain largely confined in digital environments.
